# UC-12.4.17 · GitLab Environment Tier Production Promotion Without Protected Pipeline

## Description

Production deploys should run only from protected branches and protected environments.

## Value

Reduces risk of unreviewed configuration reaching production GitOps targets.

## Implementation

Ensure deployment JSON includes environment tier; join to `gitlab:audit` for approval if available. Alert on any successful prod deploy missing protection flags.

## SPL

```spl
index=devops sourcetype="gitlab:pipeline" environment=production OR environment_name=production OR deployment_tier=production
| eval protected_pipeline=if(match(lower(_raw),"protected") OR protected_environment="true" OR only_protected_branches="true",1,0)
| where protected_pipeline=0 AND status="success"
| table _time, project_path, user_name, ref, status, environment
| sort -_time
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Change.All_Changes
  by All_Changes.user All_Changes.object All_Changes.action span=1h
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Table (recent prod deploys), Pie chart (protected vs unprotected).

## References

- [GitLab Add-on for Splunk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/6848)
- [GitLab CI/CD logs to Splunk (Lantern)](https://lantern.splunk.com/Data_Descriptors/GitLab/Getting_Gitlab_CICD_data_out_of_a_Gitlab_Pipeline_into_Splunk)
